sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Boka Expands Nationwide Across All 4,500 Walmart Stores With New Sensitivity Toothpaste and End-Cap
feed.summary: Boka, a science-backed personal care brand, has expanded its nationwide presence into all 4,500 Walmart stores across the U.S. This expansion includes a new line of sensitivity toothpaste, whitening products, and kids' oral care options, all featuring Boka's signature nano-hydroxyapatite (n-Ha) technology. The move aims to make fluoride-free oral wellness accessible to more households, building on a successful debut and an end-cap promotion in nearly 2,500 locations.

feed.title: RTX to release first quarter earnings results on April 21, 2026
feed.summary: RTX (NYSE: RTX) announced it will release its first-quarter 2026 earnings results on Tuesday, April 21, prior to the stock market opening. A conference call is scheduled for 8:30 a.m. ET to discuss these results, and it will be webcast live on the company's website. RTX, a company with over 180,000 global employees and 2025 sales exceeding $88 billion, is headquartered in Arlington, Virginia.

feed.title: Deere expands 8-series lineup with redesigned tractor models
feed.summary: John Deere has expanded its 8-series lineup by launching six new 8R and 8RX tractor models, featuring increased horsepower options up to 540 and advanced technology. These redesigned models emphasize improved performance, maneuverability, and versatility, incorporating precision agriculture tools like the G5Plus display and autonomy-ready capabilities. Enhancements also include a more powerful JD14 engine, hydraulic updates, an improved suspension system, and numerous operator comfort features, all aimed at boosting productivity for large-scale operations.

feed.title: FactSet Research Systems Inc. 2026 Q2 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation (NYSE:FDS) 2026-03-31
feed.summary: FactSet Research Systems Inc. published a slide deck in conjunction with its Q2 2026 earnings call. This presentation provides details relevant to the financial results for the quarter. The article highlights that SA Transcripts, Seeking Alpha's transcripts team, is responsible for this content.

feed.title: Franklin Templeton reshapes client unit with 3 leadership moves
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton has announced key leadership appointments to its Global Client Group, including Brett Mossman as Head of U.S. Product, Lyenda Delp as Head of Global Insurance and Institutional Asset Solutions, and Kim Roy as COO of the Global Client Group. These strategic moves aim to streamline client engagement, accelerate growth, and enhance the firm's ability to deliver tailored solutions across global markets. The appointments are part of Franklin Templeton's initiative to integrate its client experience and scale high-growth opportunities.

feed.title: IBM backs new ETH Zurich posts in 10-year AI and quantum push
feed.summary: IBM and ETH Zurich have announced a 10-year collaboration to advance foundational algorithms for the AI and quantum era. This partnership will fund joint research, create new professorships at ETH Zurich, and focus on hybrid algorithmic paradigms across four key technical areas. The goal is to bridge classical, AI-driven, and quantum computation to solve complex problems in science and industry.

feed.title: Generation Investment Management Sold Gartner (IT) Due to Fear of AI Disruption
feed.summary: Generation Investment Management revealed in its Q4 2025 investor letter that it sold its stake in Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT) due to concerns about AI's potential impact on demand for Gartner's services. The firm noted that the market currently penalizes strong companies not playing the "beat and raise" game, and while Gartner isn't among the top hedge fund picks, AI stocks are seen as having greater promise for returns. Generation Investment Management believes that AI will provide long-term value, with global computing demands expected to triple.

feed.title: Wall Street Thinks Western Digital Stock Price Will Soar Higher This Year
feed.summary: Bernstein upgraded Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) stock from Market Perform to Outperform, doubling its price target to $340, despite recent algorithmic selling fears related to Google’s TurboQuant report. The firm believes the report will have minimal impact on demand and that the selloff presents an attractive entry point, citing Western Digital's strong Q2 FY2026 earnings, robust free cash flow, and accelerating fundamentals driven by AI demand. With a consensus analyst target of $321 and strong earnings beats, Wall Street analysts largely support the bullish outlook for WDC.

feed.title: Chevron names new safety chief, refinery director
feed.summary: Chevron Corporation has announced two key leadership appointments effective May 1, 2026. Daniel Woodall will become the new Chief Health, Safety and Environment Officer, while current Chief HSE Officer Marissa Badenhorst will transition to director of the Pascagoula and Pasadena refineries. These appointments come as Chevron's stock trades near its 52-week high, with financial analysis suggesting it is currently overvalued.

feed.title: Wall Street Tells Scared Investors To Buy Verizon Stock
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for Verizon (VZ) to $47 from $43, maintaining an Equal Weight rating, highlighting the stock's defensive appeal amid high market volatility. Despite pricing pressures in wireless and broadband, Verizon's stable recurring revenues, strong financial performance—including 18 consecutive quarters of wireless service revenue growth and significant free cash flow supporting its dividend—make it attractive to investors seeking lower-beta names during uncertain economic periods. The analyst's move signals institutional rotation into defensive stocks, even with VZ currently trading above the revised target.

feed.title: Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) Upgraded by Sanford C. Bernstein to Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Sanford C. Bernstein upgraded Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) to an "Outperform" rating with a $340 price target, suggesting a 35.1% upside. This upgrade follows Western Digital's strong quarterly earnings, where the company surpassed EPS and revenue expectations. Despite the positive analyst sentiment, the stock opened down, and insider selling activity was noted, though institutional ownership remains high at 92.5%.

feed.title: Veralto Announces Acquisition of GlobalVision and the Completion of $300 Million of Share Repurchases
feed.summary: Veralto (NYSE: VLTO) has announced its acquisition of GlobalVision, a company specializing in AI-augmented quality and compliance solutions for packaging. This strategic acquisition aims to enhance Veralto's Esko business by integrating GlobalVision's technology to help pharmaceutical and consumer packaged goods customers accelerate market speed and maintain regulatory compliance. Additionally, Veralto completed approximately $300 million in share repurchases during the first quarter of 2026, representing about 1.3% of its outstanding shares.

feed.title: Crown Castle (NYSE:CCI) Now Covered by Analysts at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has initiated coverage on Crown Castle (NYSE:CCI) with a "hold" rating and a $90 price target, suggesting a 12.99% upside. This new rating adds to a mixed analyst consensus of "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $99.34, following recent price target cuts from other firms. Insiders have also recently sold shares, while institutional investors hold a significant portion of the company's stock.

feed.title: Nuwellis reports pediatric segment reaches 50% of U.S. revenue By Investing.com
feed.summary: Nuwellis, Inc. announced that its pediatric segment now accounts for 50% of its total U.S. revenue, making it the company's largest and fastest-growing segment. Despite a 97% stock decline over the past year, the medical device company has expanded its pediatric footprint to 47 centers, including six of the top 10 children's hospitals. Analyzing the company's financials shows that while Nuwellis has a 62% gross profit margin, it remains unprofitable, though InvestingPro data suggests it may be undervalued with analysts forecasting 9% revenue growth for fiscal 2026.

feed.title: News | RTX's Pratt & Whitney awarded $6.6 billion F135 production contract to definitize lots 18-19
feed.summary: Pratt & Whitney, an RTX business, has been awarded a $6.6 billion contract modification for lots 18-19 of the F135 engine, which powers the F-35 Lightning II. The contract covers full-rate production engines, spares, modules, engineering, and program support, ensuring continuous manufacturing and supply chain operations for domestic and international customers. Pratt & Whitney has invested significantly to increase production rates by 20% and has delivered over 1,400 F135 engines.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Salesforce (CRM), Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and Oracle (ORCL)
feed.summary: Analysts have issued bullish sentiments on Salesforce (CRM), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), and Oracle (ORCL). Salesforce received a Buy rating from Cantor Fitzgerald with a $300 price target, while Wells Fargo maintained a Buy rating on Palo Alto Networks with a $200 price target. Bernstein also maintained a Buy rating on Oracle, setting a price target of $319.

feed.title: Rockwell Automation Downgraded by Jefferies, Key Price Target Drops to $380
feed.summary: Jefferies downgraded Rockwell Automation (NYSE: ROK) from Buy to Hold, setting a new price target of $380, down from $490, due to concerns over investor anxiety regarding AI disintermediation potentially limiting the stock's re-rating. Despite strong operational execution by Rockwell Automation, including solid Q1 FY2026 results and growth in its Software & Control segment, the firm believes the premium valuation already reflects the company's strengths, and the risk of AI-driven disruption in traditional control systems could cap future upside. The downgrade highlights a tension between Rockwell's current performance and long-term structural risks associated with evolving AI capabilities in industrial automation.

feed.title: NextNRG to Launch the EzShop, Powered by Gopuff, to Deliver Both Fuel & Groceries Directly to Customers
feed.summary: NextNRG is launching EzShop, a new service within its EzFill mobile fueling platform, in partnership with Gopuff. This integration will allow customers to order over 5,000 everyday essentials, including groceries and household items, alongside their fuel delivery from a single app. The partnership aims to transform NextNRG into a digital commerce platform, creating a new revenue stream and enhancing customer convenience.

feed.title: Fuel at the curb, 5,000 grocery items in 15 minutes via NextNRG
feed.summary: NextNRG (NASDAQ: NXXT) has launched EzShop, an eCommerce integration within its EzFill mobile fueling app, powered by Gopuff. This new service will offer over 5,000 grocery and household items for delivery in as fast as 15 minutes, alongside fuel delivery. The initiative aims to create a new revenue stream for NextNRG by transforming its mobile fueling service into a digital commerce platform, with an initial rollout in select Q2 2026 markets and further expansion planned.

feed.title: Veralto (NYSE:VLTO) Coverage Initiated at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has initiated coverage on Veralto (NYSE:VLTO) with a "buy" rating and a $110 price target, suggesting a potential upside of 28.03%. The company recently beat Q4 EPS estimates and provided strong FY2026 guidance. Veralto currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with a target price of $108.78.

feed.title: Global Helium-Free MRI Scanners Market to Reach USD 4.2 Billion by 2036, Driven by Siemens Healthineers, Philips, and GE Healthcare Innovation
feed.summary: The global helium-free MRI scanners market is projected to grow from USD 1.8 billion in 2026 to USD 4.2 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 8.8%. This growth is driven by increasing demand for sustainable imaging infrastructure, reduced operational costs, and simplified installation compared to traditional helium-dependent MRI systems. Siemens Healthineers, Philips, and GE Healthcare are key players innovating in this evolving market, with 1.5T systems dominating and hospitals being the leading end-users, especially in regions like India and China.

feed.title: United Therapeutics Gets Dual Target Hikes From BofA and Wells Fargo
feed.summary: United Therapeutics (NASDAQ: UTHR) saw its stock surge over 12% after positive TETON-1 trial data for Tyvaso triggered price target increases from BofA and Wells Fargo. The data validates Tyvaso's potential expansion into idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF), significantly broadening its addressable market beyond pulmonary arterial hypertension. While analysts raised targets, they maintained neutral ratings, indicating conviction in the pipeline but caution on valuation after the sharp rally.

feed.title: American Tower (NYSE:AMT) Now Covered by Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has initiated coverage on American Tower (NYSE:AMT) with a "Buy" rating and a $205 price target, indicating a potential 20.5% upside. This follows American Tower's recent earnings report, which missed EPS estimates but showed revenue growth and strong FY 2026 guidance. The report also highlights recent insider trading activity and significant institutional ownership in the company.

feed.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte Stock: Key Insights for North American Investors on Business Mo
feed.summary: Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte (OMA) operates 13 airports in central-northern Mexico, offering North American investors exposure to Mexico's aviation recovery and economic ties. Its business model, driven by aeronautical tariffs and non-aeronautical services, provides stable revenue. The company is strategically expanding capacity and leveraging nearshoring trends, with its stock trading as NASDAQ-listed ADRs (OMAB).

feed.title: Nio to hit 90,000th delivery of third-gen ES8 this week
feed.summary: Nio is set to reach its 90,000th delivery of the third-generation ES8 this week, indicating strong sales momentum in March with nearly 20,000 units delivered for the month. The company plans to launch a five-seat version in early July to maintain sales and is preparing for the flagship ES9 SUV launch in Q2. Nio recently reported its first-ever quarterly GAAP operating profit in Q4 2025, largely driven by the ES8's success.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line debuts Norwegian Luna in Miami
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line has christened and debuted its newest ship, the Norwegian Luna, in Miami. The ship will offer round-trip Caribbean cruises from April 2026 through April 2027, featuring itineraries that include NCL’s private destinations, Great Stirrup Cay and Harvest Caye. The Norwegian Luna, part of the Prima Class, boasts family-friendly attractions, adult-only experiences, and diverse dining and entertainment options, designed to cater to multigenerational travelers.

feed.title: Socket Mobile Raises $500,000 via 10% Secured Subordinated Convertible Notes at $0.90 Conversion
feed.summary: Socket Mobile has secured $500,000 in financing through 10% secured subordinated convertible notes, intended to boost its working capital. These three-year notes carry a 10% annual interest, are convertible at $0.90 per share, and mature on March 27, 2029. The agreement is backed by company assets and is subordinated to Western Alliance Bank, with plans for a resale registration statement filing by June 30, 2026.

feed.title: $13M lawsuit threatens rural broadband network as American Tower seeks equipment removal
feed.summary: American Tower has filed a $13 million lawsuit against AtLink Services LLC, doing business as AtLink Services, alleging breach of contract and seeking the removal of AtLink's equipment from its cell towers in Oklahoma. This action could severely disrupt broadband access for tens of thousands of rural customers. The dispute stems from AtLink's alleged failure to make monthly payments under a 2021 agreement, and the financial ramifications are significant, especially as AtLink was acquired by Element8 in a membership interest purchase.

feed.title: SPDR SSGA My2031 Corporate Bond ETF declares monthly distribution of $0.0966
feed.summary: The SPDR SSGA My2031 Corporate Bond ETF has declared a monthly distribution of $0.0966 per share. This distribution will be paid to shareholders of record as of a specified date.

feed.title: U.S. Bank taking over Amazon's small-business card program from American Express
feed.summary: U.S. Bank is replacing American Express as the provider for Amazon's small-business credit card program, partnering with Mastercard for the co-branded cards. This move means the Minneapolis-based bank will now issue the widely used cards to Amazon's small-business customers. The change was reported by the Minneapolis / St. Paul Business Journal.

feed.title: Microsoft's cloud licensing practices draw fresh UK scrutiny
feed.summary: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is investigating Microsoft's business software ecosystem for potential "strategic market status" (SMS) designation, which could lead to stricter competition rules. The probe, set to begin in May, aims to address concerns about Microsoft's software licensing reducing competition in the cloud sector and to ensure a level playing field amidst AI-driven innovation. This move follows an earlier CMA cloud market report that prompted Microsoft and Amazon to make changes regarding interoperability and cloud egress fees, though further actions are expected from both companies.

feed.title: Therabody Scales Ulta Beauty Partnership to 1,000+ Doors Nationwide
feed.summary: Therabody is significantly expanding its partnership with Ulta Beauty, increasing its distribution to over 1,000 stores nationwide from an initial 300+, marking a threefold scale-up. This expansion, driven by strong consumer demand for science-backed wellness and beauty technology, will feature Therabody's top-performing beauty devices like the TheraFace Mask Glo and TheraFace Depuffing Wand, along with the in-store debut of Theragun Relief. The move reinforces Therabody's leadership in the self-care category and Ulta Beauty's commitment to offering innovative, high-performance wellness solutions to its customers.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Announces Webcast of First Quarter Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) is set to release its first-quarter 2026 earnings before the market opens on Tuesday, April 21, 2026. A conference call hosted by CEO Hal Lawton and CFO Kurt Barton will follow at 10 a.m. ET on the same day, featuring a live webcast at IR.TractorSupply.com. Supplemental materials will be available 15 minutes prior to the call, and a replay will be accessible online afterward.

feed.title: Nvidia Just Handed Marvell Technology the Ultimate Buy Signal
feed.summary: Nvidia has significantly boosted Marvell Technology (MRVL) with a $2 billion direct investment and a deeper AI partnership, signaling confidence in Marvell’s role in next-generation AI infrastructure. This investment follows Marvell’s strong fiscal 2026 performance, including 42% revenue growth and the acquisition of Celestial AI, which adds crucial photonic fabric technology. The endorsement validates Marvell’s differentiated position in the data-center infrastructure layer, making it an attractive option for investors looking beyond pure GPU plays in the AI sector.

feed.title: Once Upon a Farm brings refrigerated meat baby pouches to stores
feed.summary: Once Upon a Farm (OFRM) is launching five new protein-focused product lines in early April 2026, marking its largest portfolio expansion to date. These include refrigerated meat and meat & bone broth baby pouches, legume blends, smoothies with protein and probiotics, and Power Wheels snack bars. The products, all USDA Organic and non-GMO, aim to support children's nutritional needs from infancy through school age, with meat pouches providing at least 4g of protein.

feed.title: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) Now Covered by Analysts at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has initiated coverage on Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) with a "hold" rating and a $310 price target, suggesting a 15.25% upside. This follows Keysight's recent earnings beat and positive FY2026 guidance, while other analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus. Despite high institutional ownership, recent insider selling has been noted.

feed.title: SBA Communications (NASDAQ:SBAC) Earns Hold Rating from Analysts at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial initiated coverage on SBA Communications (NASDAQ:SBAC) with a "hold" rating and a $193 price target, indicating a potential 13.6% upside. This new rating aligns with the broader analyst consensus of "Hold" for SBAC, which has an average target price of $230.11. The company recently reported mixed results with stronger-than-expected EPS but slightly missed revenue estimates.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial Begins Coverage on NexMetals Mining (NASDAQ:NEXM)
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has initiated coverage on NexMetals Mining (NASDAQ:NEXM) with an "Outperform" rating, while Weiss Ratings reiterated a "sell (e+)", resulting in a MarketBeat consensus of "Hold". The stock opened at $2.27, near its 52-week low, and has a market capitalization of $80.6 million. NexMetals Mining has reported a ($0.22) EPS and exhibits strong liquidity with a quick/current ratio of 4.67, but a negative P/E ratio of -1.79.

feed.title: PACCAR (NASDAQ:PCAR) Coverage Initiated at BNP Paribas Exane
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Exane has initiated coverage on PACCAR (NASDAQ:PCAR) with a "neutral" rating and a $126 price target, implying a 12.03% potential upside. The broader analyst consensus for PACCAR is a "Hold" with an average price target of $117. The company recently reported quarterly EPS of $1.06, meeting estimates, and revenue of $6.82 billion, exceeding expectations despite a 15.1% year-over-year decrease.

feed.title: Why Coca-Cola is kosher for Passover (thanks to one rabbi)
feed.summary: Nearly a century ago, Rabbi Tobias Geffen worked with Coca-Cola to create a kosher-for-Passover version of their popular soda, making it accessible to observant Jews. Initially, standard Coke was not kosher for Passover due to high fructose corn syrup and an enzyme from barley. Geffen's efforts led to formulation changes, including the use of cane sugar and coconut/cottonseed oil-derived glycerin, allowing Coca-Cola to be certified kosher for Passover and establishing a precedent for major companies accommodating religious dietary laws.

feed.title: General Mills unveils new classic cereal varieties
feed.summary: General Mills has launched two new cereal varieties: Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy Cereal and Tropical Trix featuring Disney’s Moana Cereal. Both new products are made with colors from natural sources, aligning with General Mills' commitment to remove certified colors from its US cereal portfolio by summer 2026 and its full US retail portfolio by the end of 2027. These new cereals will be available at US retailers starting this summer.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of Oracle Corporation Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between June 12, 2025, and December 16, 2025. Investors are encouraged to contact the firm to discuss their legal rights, as Oracle's stock price experienced significant drops following announcements of a large cloud computing contract with OpenAI, plans for substantial debt sales for AI buildout, and a revised, much higher CapEx forecast.

feed.title: BMS links up with Faro to use AI to improve clinical trial protocols
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) has partnered with Faro in a multi-year agreement to integrate AI into its clinical trial protocols. This collaboration aims to enhance trial design, drafting, validation, and optimization processes, leveraging Faro's expertise in digital protocols and AI application. The partnership supports both early- and late-phase programs and is part of BMS's broader strategy to utilize AI across its clinical development, drug development, and diagnostics efforts, including previous agreements with Evinova and Microsoft.

feed.title: OST Shareholder Alert: Ostin Technology Group Co., Ltd. Securities Class Action Lawsuit Investors With Losses May Join -- The Gross Law Firm
feed.summary: The Gross Law Firm has issued a shareholder alert for Ostin Technology Group Co., Ltd. (NASDAQ: OST) investors who experienced losses during the class period of May 11, 2025, to June 26, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that defendants conspired to artificially inflate OST's stock price through a registered direct offering and warrant exchange agreement. Investors are encouraged to contact the firm, with the deadline to seek lead plaintiff appointment being April 17, 2026.

feed.title: APO Shareholder Alert: Apollo Global management, Inc. Securities Class Action Lawsuit Investors With Losses May Join -- The Gross Law Firm
feed.summary: The Gross Law Firm has issued a shareholder alert for investors of Apollo Global Management, Inc. (NYSE: APO). This alert concerns a securities class action lawsuit for shares purchased between May 10, 2021, and February 21, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Apollo Global's leadership frequently communicated with Jeffrey Epstein and falsely claimed not to have done business with him, leading to potential harm to the company's reputation.

feed.title: International Paper to discuss market conditions after Apr. 30 results
feed.summary: International Paper (NYSE: IP) will release its first-quarter 2026 earnings on April 30, 2026, before the New York Stock Exchange opens. The company will host a webcast at 10:00 a.m. ET (9:00 a.m. CT) to discuss these earnings and current market conditions, with both live participation and a 90-day audio replay available. Historically, similar earnings date announcements from International Paper have resulted in minimal stock price movement, around -0.23%.

feed.title: IT Shareholder Alert: May 18, 2026 Lead Plaintiff Deadline in Gartner, Inc. Securities Class Action Lawsuit -- The Gross Law Firm
feed.summary: The Gross Law Firm has issued a shareholder alert for investors of Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) regarding a securities class action lawsuit. Investors who purchased shares between February 4, 2025, and February 2, 2026, are encouraged to contact the firm, as the deadline to seek lead plaintiff appointment is May 18, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Gartner made materially false and misleading statements regarding its growth rates and ability to meet consulting revenue targets.

feed.title: Teledyne Marine Wins UK Royal Navy Contract for AUVs and Floats ​
feed.summary: Teledyne Marine has secured a contract with the UK Ministry of Defence to supply the Royal Navy with autonomous ocean observing systems, including Sentinel and Slocum gliders and APEX floats. This initiative, part of the Future Maritime Data Gathering program, aims to enhance the Royal Navy's capabilities in collecting high-quality oceanographic and environmental data. The contract reinforces Teledyne's long-standing support for the Royal Navy and the increasing role of unmanned systems in naval operations.

feed.title: Robert Taubman, shopping mall scion, on his late rival turned friend
feed.summary: Robert Taubman, a prominent figure in the shopping mall industry, reflects on his relationship with a former rival who later became a friend. The article, published in Crain's Detroit Business, includes a photo of David Simon, chairman and CEO of Simon Property Group Inc., speaking at a 2019 summit. This article was published on March 31, 2026, at 09:00 AM EDT.

feed.title: ENPH Shareholder Alert: Enphase Energy, Inc. Securities Class Action Lawsuit Investors With Losses May Join -- The Gross Law Firm
feed.summary: The Gross Law Firm has issued a shareholder alert for Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH), announcing a securities class action lawsuit. Investors who purchased ENPH shares between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025, are encouraged to join the lawsuit, which alleges that the company made materially false and misleading statements regarding its ability to manage inventory and mitigate effects from the termination of the 25D Credit. The deadline to seek lead plaintiff appointment is April 20, 2026.
